about.title: About burnmark
about.intro: burnmark is a free, open-source label designer that runs entirely in your browser. No account, no server, no install.
about.nameBody: Thermal printers work by burning tiny marks onto heat-sensitive paper. Each dot on your label is literally a burn mark — a precise point of heat from the print head. We thought that was a pretty cool name for a label app.
about.originBody: It started with a messy workshop and a Dymo LabelManager that refused to cooperate on Linux. Instead of organising the workshop, we ended up reverse-engineering USB printer protocols and accidentally building an entire label printing ecosystem. The workshop is still not fully organised.
about.projectBody: burnmark is part of the thermal-label ecosystem — open-source drivers for Dymo and Brother label printers, a headless design engine, and this web app. Everything is MIT-licensed.
about.supportBody: burnmark is built and maintained by Mannes Brak in the Netherlands. If you find it useful, consider supporting the project:

help.title: Help
help.restartTourHint: Re-runs the welcome tour from step 1.
help.shortcutsHint: Speed up everything with the keyboard.
help.compatibilityHint: Which printers work, and which browsers they need.
help.docsHint: How-tos and protocol notes at thermal-label.github.io.
help.compatIntro: burnmark works best in Chrome or Edge.
help.compatChrome: Chrome (desktop and Android): full support — WebUSB, Web Serial, PWA.
help.compatFirefox: Firefox: design and export only — no printing (WebUSB not supported).
help.compatSafari: Safari: design and export only — no printing.
help.compatBrother: Brother QL series (QL-800, QL-810W, QL-820NWB, QL-1100, QL-1110NWB)
help.compatConnectBody: USB cable or Bluetooth (Brother QL-820NWB via Web Serial). Android users: a USB-C OTG adapter (€5) connects directly.

tour.completedToast: You're ready! Need help later? Check the help menu.
tour.step1Body: This is a sample — click any element to edit, or start fresh.
tour.step2Body: Text, images, barcodes, shapes — pick one and click the label.
tour.step3Title: Edit objects in the side panel
tour.step3Body: Click any object in the list to edit its properties.
tour.step4Body: Connect your printer and hit print. That's it!
